objective_taxonomy.name: connectivityOf
objective_taxonomy.definition: Refers to the ability to traverse the network; focuses on reachability and the existence of paths rather than resilience.

objective_taxonomy.name: robustnessOf
objective_taxonomy.definition: Refers to the network's ability to maintain its structural integrity and function when nodes or edges are removed or fail.

objective_taxonomy.name: centralityOf
objective_taxonomy.definition: Measures the relative importance, influence, or structural prominence of a specific entity within the network topology.

objective_taxonomy.name: densityOf
objective_taxonomy.definition: Measures the ratio of actual edges present in the object relative to the maximum possible number of edges.

objective_taxonomy.name: cohesivenessOf
objective_taxonomy.definition: Focuses on the internal strength and bonding of a single group; measures how tightly-knit and difficult to separate the object is.

objective_taxonomy.name: clusteringOf
objective_taxonomy.definition: Measures the tendency for nodes to form dense local neighborhoods or triangles (triadic closure).

objective_taxonomy.name: sizeOf
objective_taxonomy.definition: Quantifies the magnitude of the object, typically defined by the count of its nodes (order) or edges.

objective_taxonomy.name: modularityOf
objective_taxonomy.definition: Measures the quality of a specific partition of the network; compares the density of intra-community links to what would be expected in a random network.

objective_taxonomy.name: balanceOf
objective_taxonomy.definition: Measures how evenly structural load or connectivity is distributed across the graph, aiming to avoid concentration in a few nodes or links.

objective_taxonomy.name: assortativityOf
objective_taxonomy.definition: Measures the tendency of nodes to connect to other similar nodes based on an attribute like degree (homophily).

objective_taxonomy.name: hierarchyOf
objective_taxonomy.definition: Measures the extent to which the object displays a stratified, flow-oriented, or tree-like structure with inherent directionality.

objective_taxonomy.name: distanceBetween
objective_taxonomy.definition: Measures the length of the shortest path (geodesic) connecting two specific objects.

objective_taxonomy.name: connectivityBetween
objective_taxonomy.definition: Refers to the number, capacity, or resilience of paths between two objects; determines how well-connected they are rather than just how far apart they are.

objective_taxonomy.name: similarityBetween
objective_taxonomy.definition: Measures the degree to which two objects exhibit structural or role equivalence; determines whether entities behave similarly within the topology.
